_meta.purpose: Concrete external-source values discovered for the BDBV 2026 outbreak, mapped to the levers in catalog.json. Staging input for the post-2026-06-19 scored refresh and for run_local sensitivity. Does NOT modify the frozen 2026-05-20 snapshot.
_meta.licensing: CC-BY-4.0 covers this schema and the Arcede annotations. Quoted/extracted third-party figures remain under their source license (WHO CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO; IOM DTM per IOM terms; Wikipedia CC BY-SA 4.0).
_meta.provenance_convention: source_id values mirror data/bundibugyo-2026/manifest.json (canonical form, without the manifest's -live suffix). retrieved_at is the discovery timestamp for this compilation.

staged_observations.publisher: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
staged_observations.inclusion_basis: DRC and Uganda Ministries of Health figures summarized by CDC; includes two imported Uganda confirmed cases.
exclusions: No US cases confirmed because of this outbreak
staged_observations.note: Exact CDC tuple; keep separate from WHO speech approximate suspected count.

staged_observations.publisher: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
staged_observations.inclusion_basis: CDC summary of DRC and Uganda Ministries of Health figures.
exclusions: Do not fold into suspected unless a downstream model explicitly consumes non-confirmed burden.
staged_observations.note: Separate denominator; not interchangeable with suspected.

staged_observations.publisher: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
staged_observations.inclusion_basis: CDC summary of DRC and Uganda Ministries of Health figures; includes two imported Uganda cases.
exclusions: American evacuated to Germany is context-only unless an official source states it is included in DRC/Uganda count.
staged_observations.note: Use as exact May 19 tuple, not as May 20 confirmed endpoint.

staged_observations.publisher: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
staged_observations.inclusion_basis: CDC summary of DRC and Uganda Ministries of Health figures.
exclusions: Do not treat as confirmed deaths.

staged_observations.inclusion_basis: WHO DG remarks: 51 confirmed DRC cases in Ituri/North Kivu and two confirmed Kampala cases.
exclusions: American evacuated to Germany is reported separately from DRC/Uganda confirmed total.
staged_observations.note: Best official confirmed endpoint for 20 May, but not a full structured case-status tuple.

staged_observations.inclusion_basis: WHO DG remarks phrase this as 'almost 600 suspected cases'.
exclusions: Do not treat as exact numeric input without approximate/bounded semantics.

staged_observations.inclusion_basis: WHO DG remarks report 139 suspected deaths.
exclusions: Do not treat as confirmed deaths.
staged_observations.note: Potential May 20 death endpoint; keep separate from CDC May 19 tuple.

exclusions: Do not use as primary when same-day official WHO/CDC metrics exist.
staged_observations.note: Retained only as a reconciled-range/cross-check input.

sources.license: IOM DTM terms (attribution; confirm before redistribution)

sources.publisher: TradeMark Africa / Govt of Uganda + DRC OSBP documentation (via data/zones.json mahagi-cod entry)

admin2_movement_share.comment: Share of assessed Ituri movement by territory of origin (IOM DTM Ituri, round 13). Higher share = higher onward-movement propensity from zones in that territory. Territories not listed had no published share in the round-13 summary (treated as no information).

border_crossings.note: Mahagi/Goli One-Stop Border Post, one of DRC's busiest Northern Corridor crossings to NW Uganda (Arua). Documented in data/zones.json mahagi-cod entry.

edge_weight_recipe.crossing_factor: 1.3 for border crossings flagged intensity=high; 1.0 otherwise
edge_weight_recipe.nature: Transparent first-pass heuristic to map surveyed movement onto corridor edge weights. NOT fitted. Used for run_local sensitivity only; a calibrated covariate weighting is a refresh-time decision.

confirmation_latency.assay_mismatch: Initial 20 samples collected in Rwampara HZ and run on the Bunia Provincial Public Health Laboratory GeneXpert (Zaire-specific Ebola Xpert) returned negative; confirmation required shipment to INRB Kinshasa for PCR, which confirmed Orthoebolavirus (Bundibugyo) on 2026-05-15.

datapoints_days.what: Uganda case: sample collection on admission -> result

confirmation_latency.implication: Reporting completeness is lower and publication latency longer than a Zaire-EVD baseline because the frontline assay did not detect BDBV. This raises the visibility-adjustment factor (1 / reporting_completeness_lower_50) and therefore the visibility-adjusted corridor risk. A refresh should lower the reporting-completeness prior accordingly.
confirmation_latency.numeric_per_sample_distribution: partner_only (INRB / provincial lab turnaround line list)

outstanding.name: Nyankunde
outstanding.reason: New affected HZ in WHO AFRO SitRep 01 (data as of 18 May 2026): 14 suspected, 4 confirmed. Needs an RGC.cd/GRID3 polygon centroid before mapping (no fabricated coordinate). Approximate location: Irumu territory, south of Bunia.

outstanding.name: Katwa
outstanding.reason: New affected HZ in WHO AFRO SitRep 01 (data as of 18 May 2026): 1 confirmed. Needs an RGC.cd/GRID3 polygon centroid before mapping (no fabricated coordinate). Adjacent to Butembo.

centroids.method_reference: DRC health zones: polygon centroid from RGC.cd / GRID3 shapefile (as done for Rwampara). Uganda districts: admin-HQ town coordinate (as done for Arua, Kasese, Bundibugyo).

counts_escalation.staging_note: For the POST-2026-06-19 refresh, NOT the frozen 2026-05-20 snapshot. The 2026-05-20 snapshot is pinned at confirmed=53 / suspected=653 / deaths=144 across 3 HZ.

as_of_2026_05_20_to_21.note: Geographic expansion well beyond the 3 pinned HZ; Goma (North Kivu) confirmed 2026-05-17. Numeric per-zone confirmed remains unpublished.

sitrep_01_2026_05_18.note: WHO AFRO authoritative per-HZ line, data as of 18 May 2026. Seven affected health zones: the 3 pinned (Mongbwalu/Rwampara/Bunia) plus Nyankunde (Ituri) and Butembo/Katwa/Goma (North Kivu). Nyankunde and Katwa are NEW vs the pinned set. Stage for the next snapshot's affected_zones expansion and per-HZ table; do NOT backfill the frozen 2026-05-20 snapshot.

corridor_signals_2026_05_18_sitrep.staging_note: Official-but-provisional corridor evidence from WHO AFRO SitRep 01. Context for the next snapshot's corridor narrative and target set; NOT a model input and NOT a confirmed-case assertion.

signals.signal: four active cross-border exposure clusters under investigation in Ntoroko District (Uganda)
signals.implication: reinforces the Ntoroko / Bundibugyo (Lake Albert) corridor and the bundibugyo-uga target

signals.signal: mobile laboratories deploying to Mongbwalu and Mahagi (described as the Ituri-Uganda border)
signals.implication: confirms Mahagi as an active Ituri-Uganda border node feeding the arua-uga axis

signals.signal: Uganda deploying a mobile laboratory to Kasese near the DRC border
Uganda regional EOCs activated in Fort Portal, Arua, Yumbe, Kampala, Kabale, Hoima
signals.implication: Arua and Yumbe (West Nile) prioritized by WHO/Uganda, supporting the arua-uga and nebbi-uga target adds
